item.title: Great but unfinished?
item.content:encoded:
Really good set up, but I feel like the Foundat would never stop here. Curiosity kills these cats. Of course they’d be sending in D-class or an MTF with video feeds (since eye contact doesn’t seem to stun thru media).

Even before this, scientists would have kept someone watching the group in person while the second two surgical experiments were conducted, wanting to see if the 41 would be forced to move even when directly observed.

Still a great start! Maybe almost done. But it’s not an SCP until maximum science has been applied (and perhaps failed).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking the same. Anyways +1 for the creeps!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can huddle with us
We've got everything you need forty-two anoxically preserved corpses
Huddle with us
We are free trapped in a mislabelled basement in New England
P-p-p-pose with us
I see passion terror and paralysis in your eyes
Huddle with us
(ooooooh…)

When you're behind closed doors,
All alone by yourself with forty one identical clones,
And you're longing inside,
To be somebody else.
You pick up the telephone,
And there's no one on the line but can't call anyone because your mouth's sewn shut,
Don't be afraid,
'Cause there's still time you can still engineer to be contained by the Foundation and perform endless pieces of group mime for them to encourage them to stick around and keep you company.

yeah this is completely impossible to sing in time, sue me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking this as well tbh… though it's possible they were born XO-female but were completely neutered before puberty. Then again, doing similar with an otherwise normal XX or XY individual (even moreso with, say, an XY individual with AIS) should produce a similar end result but for the genotype and the lack of non-sexual developmental abnormalities. Which the figures may have had anyway, it just wasn't mentioned and may be disguised due to their peatbog-like preservation.

A more sophisticated explanation could be that all the parts of the X chromosome that would otherwise have coded for the development of any kind of sexual organs at all has been stripped out by some anomaously effective genetic engineer (…and total genital agenesis does sometimes happen even in nature), in order to make space to insert second copies of certain codons and have them work as if a second chromosome is present, so to avoid the gross upper body deformities seen with Turner's and the like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad I ran across this. I'm a sucker for WTNV references and the creepiness of the corpses juxtaposed with the subtle sense of humor shown by their activities (up until the incident) captured the emotional flavor of Night Vale and plonked it into a wholly new setting.

Immediate headcanon: this was some necromancer's attempt to reverse engineer an Eidolonic Collective, but they had to abandon the project before it was finished. Currently picturing the Overmind working overtime trying to integrate the knowledge and biological systems of the two new nodes. Who knows, maybe they just needed a few extra components to bring themself fully on-line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Little biology nitpick.

+ Open Biology jargon
-Close Biology jargon

each instance only possesses 45 chromosomes, with the second X or Y chromosome being absent

This is an actual karyotype that regular ol' non-anomalous humans can have. It results in Turner's Syndrome, and people who have it are phenotypically female. They are sterile, have a webbed neck, low-set ears, a low hairline at the back, and are shorter than average. It happens when sister chromatid separation doesn't happen properly during meiosis of the gametes, so you either have a sperm missing its sex chromosome or you have an egg missing it (but in this case, the sperm must have had an X chromosome, because YO is not a viable genotype). A better way to do it would be to say that the SCP-4309 instances have only 44 chromosomes, and that they lack sex chromosomes. Technically, this isn't a viable result, but its more believable than them being androgynous despite having the karyotype for Turner's Syndrome.


That little tidbit notwithstanding, I enjoyed the article a lot. Reminds me of those corpses in the shipping crate from phase event 52J-O79-3GH of SCP-507. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget 4099.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s the ones with a reference to “SCP Foundation Department of Abnormalities” like 3790 and 4220. They’re usually about sone type of containment chamber.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna sound like a zoomer, but what the hecke! Is a DOA article
It's Dead Or Alive, innit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree the DOA reference was unnecessary. DOA articles have a certain air of mystery and unease that this article, while creepy, didn’t quite have.

That aside, I really like the anomaly and the story the article tells, so it’s an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really light, fun little read. Is it per chance the same anomaly SCP-507 encounters in one of its logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the feeling it gives me. Like a jump scare while reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes and no. They didn't become dangerous until someone opened their eyes, but someone likely would have come along eventually, and they may not have been able to contain SCP-4309 as well as the Foundation did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Remove the (somewhat forced) DOA connection, and I like this a lot. To be honest, I feel the test logs could be done away with altogether as well, but that may just be me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a bad article, just a bad DOA article. A good DOA article provides hints of conspiracy and promotes an atmosphere of unease. It makes you think whatever was locked away was locked away for a reason and should not be bothered at all. This doesn't hit those marks - if anything, the Foundation experimenting on them so much provides the opposite feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit too much here for my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loving the 19th century gothic horror vibe, here

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand the violent brawl and casting lots positions, but other than that I like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creative and creepy! +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, I'm imagining this looking kinda like… Gibdos at the Bottom Of The Well in Kakariko Village or something (odd reference?)

But, to the story, I like the way it seems to start with an eerie balance of creepy / playfu,l with the enacting of these tableau scenes, then by the end tips that balance to definitely dangerous.

Question
– hide block

Is the idea that the threat was ultimately brought on by the research itself? Seems like these bodies were keen to just stay in the pit and not bother anyone until the Foundation started doing these surgeries on them.

Anyhow, this gets my +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
"Tired of your home? Sick of comfort? Come to the Hole in the Vacant Lot out back of the Ralph’s and huddle with Us.

Who are we? Good question.

Come to the Hole in the Vacant Lot out back of the Ralph’s and huddle with Us.

Why do we want you to come? Why did we spend money for this airtime? We understand you are confused.

But: Hole, Vacant Lot, Ralph’s, huddle, Us.

For the low-low price. Act today. Or tomorrow. Not Wednesday. Wednesday is no good for Us.

Anyway, we’re almost out of airtime, so just come on down to the Hole in the Vacant Lot out back of the Ralph’s and huddle with Us.

Or else."

~ Welcome to Night Vale, Episode Five.

So, yeah, just some creepypasta monsters. T'is the season, and I'm hoping I managed to make them interesting enough to earn a few upvotes.

Thanks to my beta readers: CaptainKirby, ChrisPratten,Darkstuff, Drblackbox, IHP, stormbreath, superblooby, thebillith, and Weryllium.
